data.body: PASS 1 (asserted-capability) finding: The plan's central capability claim — that event COMPACTION will fold/absorb/expire old `REVIEW_RESULT` events to keep the sidecar log bounded — is REFUTED by the code. `REVIEW_RESULT` is a reducer-ignored type: it lives in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (`src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:77`) and is NOT in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (`_version.py:112`). Compaction at `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py:206` explicitly `continue`s past any event whose `etype not in KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, so `REVIEW_RESULT` events are never folded into a SNAPSHOT and never deleted by compaction. This is confirmed by regression coverage in `tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py:1-12` ("compaction deliberately folds ONLY KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES ... a reducer-ignored type such as REVIEW_RESULT ... is therefore correctly absent from source_event_uuids"). The single AC ("event compaction folds/absorbs old REVIEW_RESULT events so the sidecar log stays bounded") depends on a mechanism that does not apply to reducer-ignored events; the growth-bounding path needs a dedicated mechanism, not compaction. (Advisory finding, non-blocking.)
